stimulus: Two years before Susan B. Anthony became famous for casting a ballot in defiance of the laws restricting suffrage in Rochester, New York, a Norwegian American teacher named Marie Hansen was fined for the same ______blank to several local historians, Hansen’s prosecution helped focus regional press attention on the question of voting rights long before Anthony’s arrest.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. "Two years before...act" and "according to...arrest" are both independent clauses. Separating them with a period is the only grammatically correct option among the choices.

Choice A is incorrect because the coordinating conjunction "and" requires a preceding comma to join two independent clauses. Choice B is incorrect because omitting punctuation fuses two independent clauses into a run-on. Choice D is incorrect because a comma alone produces a comma splice between two independent clauses.
